notes: Apple is a technology company producing Mac computers, iPhone, iPad, and associated software and services. Apple Product Security handles vulnerability response for all Apple platforms including iOS/iPadOS (~1.5 billion active devices), macOS, Safari (WebKit engine, also used by all iOS browsers per Apple policy), watchOS, tvOS, and visionOS. Apple is a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) and delays CVE details until patches are available. Apple vulnerabilities are high-value targets for nation-states and commercial spyware vendors — iOS zero-days are among the most valuable ($1M+). Pegasus spyware notably exploited Apple zero-days. Safari/WebKit vulnerabilities affect all iOS browsers because Apple requires all iOS browsers to use WebKit.

data.description: Apple publishes security content as HT (Help Topic) support articles. Each HT article corresponds to a software release and lists all CVEs fixed in that release.
data.notes: HT articles document the security content of OS and app updates. Covers iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, Safari, visionOS, and other Apple software. Apple bundles many CVEs per release — a single HT article may reference dozens of CVE fixes. Apple provides minimal vulnerability details until fixes ship (coordinated release model). Security fixes ship bundled with OS/app updates, not as standalone patches. Apple Security Research runs a bug bounty program. The HT article listing page at support.apple.com/en-us/HT201222 provides an index of all security updates.
